topic.prompt_en: Describe a success and explain what you gained from it.
topic.learning_goal: Learn to talk about success in a grounded way that includes effort, result, and personal gain.

angles.title: Set the scene
content: Say when it happened and why it mattered at the time.
Keep the background short so you can move into the event quickly.

angles.title: Explain what happened
content: Describe the main action in a clear time order.
Choose one or two details that make the event believable.

angles.title: Show the turning point
content: Include the moment when the situation changed or became clear.
That moment usually carries the emotional weight of the answer.

angles.title: End with meaning
content: Finish with the lesson, result, or lasting impression.
A short reflection gives the story a real ending.

sequence.target: State the situation, time, and why this event belongs in your answer.
Describe what happened and where the key shift came.
End with the effect, lesson, or memory that stayed with you.

high_score_tips: Keep the event focused on one clear thread instead of listing everything that happened.
Use one turning point so the answer has shape and forward movement.
A calm reflection usually sounds stronger than exaggerated emotion.
Let the ending show why the event still matters to you now.

content.why_it_hurts: The listener loses the main story if every small detail gets equal time.
content.fix: Keep only the details that change the meaning or the outcome.

content.why_it_hurts: Without a shift, the answer sounds like a flat report instead of a real experience.
content.fix: Identify the exact moment when something became easier, harder, clearer, or more memorable.

content.why_it_hurts: The story feels unfinished if you stop right after the event ends.
content.fix: Add one sentence about what you learned or how you see it now.

language.wrong_example: I go there last week and everyone is waiting for me.
language.better_example: I went there last week and everyone was waiting for me.
language.reason: When you tell a finished event, simple past should carry the main timeline.

language.wrong_example: It was very memorable and very special.
language.better_example: It was memorable because it changed how I handled similar situations later.
language.reason: Specific explanation is stronger than repeating very.

core.example: I would naturally use "incident" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "outcome" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "detail" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "reaction" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "confidence" when talking about this topic.
I would naturally use "preparation" when talking about this topic.

extended.note: Useful when the story changes direction.
Useful when effort leads to success.

core.example: I would use "what happened was" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "at first" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "in the end" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "it taught me that" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "go smoothly" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "feel proud of" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.

extended.phrase: one thing that stood out was
extended.note: Good for the moment you want to emphasize.
extended.example: I would use "one thing that stood out was" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.

extended.note: Useful when the event caused a later change.
extended.example: I would use "from that point on" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "pay off in the end" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would use "prove to myself that" when describing a success that mattered more than the result itself.

framework.goal: Tell one event clearly through context, turning point, result, and reflection.

moves: Set the time and situation in one or two sentences.
Show why this event was worth talking about.
Make the turning point obvious and easy to follow.
State the outcome and what stayed with you.
Keep the reflection grounded and realistic.

delivery_markers: The moment that really stood out was ...
At first, I thought ... but then ...

samples.answer:
A success that mattered more than the result itself is something I can talk about quite naturally. Last year, I led a small presentation at work that I had been nervous about for weeks. I was not worried about the slides themselves; I was more worried about answering questions in front of senior people. The turning point came when I stopped trying to sound perfect and focused on being clear and useful. The presentation went smoothly, and one manager later told me the structure was easy to follow.

What made it worth talking about was not only the event or idea itself, but also the effect it had on the way I think or act. What I gained most was not praise but confidence that preparation and clarity matter more than sounding impressive. Overall, it is a topic that feels real to me because it connects to ordinary life rather than a perfect story.
highlights: Band 6 answer matched to the 描述一次成功与收获 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

opening: The topic that comes to mind is a success that mattered more than the result itself.
I would first explain the background clearly.
body: Then I would add the central detail or turning point.
After that, I would show the result or practical effect.
closing: Finally, I would end with the takeaway or present view.

upgrades.improved: It mattered because What I gained most was not praise but confidence that preparation and clarity matter more than sounding impressive.
upgrades.why: A specific reason sounds much more natural than a general claim.
upgrades.note: Use the personal effect to close the answer.

upgrades.improved: The turning point came when I stopped trying to sound perfect and focused on being clear and useful.
upgrades.why: A clear turning point gives the answer shape and movement.
upgrades.note: This is especially useful in spoken storytelling and reflective answers.

samples.answer:
The topic that comes to mind for me is a success that mattered more than the result itself. Last year, I led a small presentation at work that I had been nervous about for weeks. I was not worried about the slides themselves; I was more worried about answering questions in front of senior people. What stood out most was that the turning point came when I stopped trying to sound perfect and focused on being clear and useful.

After that, the presentation went smoothly, and one manager later told me the structure was easy to follow. What I find interesting about it now is that the situation seemed fairly ordinary at the time, but it revealed something more lasting about how I handle pressure, choices, or relationships. What I gained most was not praise but confidence that preparation and clarity matter more than sounding impressive. So if I were explaining this in a speaking test, I would focus less on sounding dramatic and more on why the experience or idea genuinely stayed with me.
highlights: Band 7 answer matched to the 描述一次成功与收获 topic
Keeps spoken rhythm while staying specific and teachable

samples.answer:
If I had to answer this topic in a more reflective way, I would still begin with a success that mattered more than the result itself. Last year, I led a small presentation at work that I had been nervous about for weeks. I was not worried about the slides themselves; I was more worried about answering questions in front of senior people. The turning point, for me, was that the turning point came when I stopped trying to sound perfect and focused on being clear and useful.

From there, the presentation went smoothly, and one manager later told me the structure was easy to follow. What matters now is not the surface event or statement alone, but the shift in perspective behind it. What I gained most was not praise but confidence that preparation and clarity matter more than sounding impressive. In that sense, the topic feels useful because it shows not only what happened or what I think, but also how my judgment has become a little more precise over time.

practice.task: Describe a success and explain what you gained from it.
checklist: Set the scene briefly before the main event.
End with the result or lesson, not just the action.
self_prompts: What changed in the middle of the story?
Why do you still remember it now?
What did you learn from it?
